title: Response Commitment, Problem Details, Streaming, and Downloads · Contract Lens: Response Commit Single Owner
summary: Handlers write success before work completes, expose stack traces, send inconsistent problem objects, ignore stream backpressure, and assume errors can replace committed headers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts response commit single owner increment in single-owner response and bounded stream protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose status and fields before commitment, assign one response owner, return after terminal sends, and observe writableEnded, headersSent, finish, close, and error distinctly.
Stop reads and downstream work on abort, remove partial artifacts, record bounded causal evidence, and test slow, truncated, malformed, oversized, and changed bodies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
